channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18047] sanity-lnet test_260: tttttwrong value: 1024 tttttexpected: 128
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Frederick Dilger <fdilger@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/0565406a-d51d-4997-9f59-25ba71059267

test_260 failed with the following error:

$'Parameter net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh1 tttttwrong value: 1024 tttttexpected: 128'

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/106219 - 4.18.0-513.24.1.el8_9.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/106219 - 4.18.0-513.24.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

<<Please provide additional information about the failure here>>
Failed 13.43% of most recent runs as of July 18th.

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
sanity-lnet test_260 - $'Parameter net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh1 tttttwrong value: 1024 tttttexpected: 128'
sanity-lnet test_260 - $'Parameter net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh2 tttttwrong value: 2048 tttttexpected: 512'
sanity-lnet test_260 - $'Parameter net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh3 tttttwrong value: 4096 tttttexpected: 1024'
sanity-lnet test_260 - Values not set

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: +1 at https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/48b08ed3-0eee-437d-a93f-b219d8e76221 which contains the landed patch.

There are some occurrences of this issue on master at https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/772a9352-7b66-4378-b204-1b41acbff7f2 and https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/21861564-ca5a-487f-bed7-3f314f954d61, on a branch that includes the landed fixes.

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56025/
Subject: LU-18047 lnet: sanity-lnet test_260 wrong value
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 9e4a92512f3c3803a555cba6668e73665b1f5701

"Frank Sehr <fsehr@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56025
Subject: LU-18047 lnet: sanity-lnet test_260 wrong value
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 89f8c32d3711550325c4e725a57da7d9094b3323
This subtest is failing quite a bit - 76 times in the past week.

[...]
Check original configuration
found: net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh1 128
found: net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh2 512
found: net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh3 1024
found: net.ipv6.neigh.default.gc_thresh1 128
found: net.ipv6.neigh.default.gc_thresh2 2048
Parameter net.ipv6.neigh.default.gc_thresh2 					wrong value: 2048 					expected: 512
found: net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh1 128
found: net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh2 512
found: net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh3 1024
found: net.ipv6.neigh.default.gc_thresh1 128
found: net.ipv6.neigh.default.gc_thresh2 2048
Parameter net.ipv6.neigh.default.gc_thresh2 					wrong value: 2048 					expected: 512
found: net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh1 128
found: net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh2 512
found: net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh3 1024
found: net.ipv6.neigh.default.gc_thresh1 128
found: net.ipv6.neigh.default.gc_thresh2 2048
Parameter net.ipv6.neigh.default.gc_thresh2 					wrong value: 2048 					expected: 512
found: net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh1 128
found: net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh2 512
found: net.ipv4.neigh.default.gc_thresh3 1024
found: net.ipv6.neigh.default.gc_thresh1 128
found: net.ipv6.neigh.default.gc_thresh2 2048
Parameter net.ipv6.neigh.default.gc_thresh2 					wrong value: 2048 					expected: 512
 sanity-lnet test_260: @@@@@@ FAIL: Values not set 
  Trace dump:
  = /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:7169:error()
  = /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity-lnet.sh:4059:test_260()
  = /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:7514:run_one()
  = /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:7577:run_one_logged()
  = /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/test-framework.sh:7400:run_test()
  = /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity-lnet.sh:4069:main()
Dumping lctl log to /autotest/autotest-2/2024-08-10/lustre-reviews_review-ldiskfs-dne_106766_56_0a59264a-c17f-4827-8aae-a370d6c3ad97//sanity-lnet.test_260.*.1723310919.log
CMD: onyx-141vm12,onyx-25vm11,onyx-25vm7.onyx.whamcloud.com,onyx-25vm8,onyx-25vm9 /usr/sbin/lctl dk > /autotest/autotest-2/2024-08-10/lustre-reviews_review-ldiskfs-dne_106766_56_0a59264a-c17f-4827-8aae-a370d6c3ad97//sanity-lnet.test_260.debug_log.\$(hostname -s).1723310919.log;
		dmesg > /autotest/autotest-2/2024-08-10/lustre-reviews_review-ldiskfs-dne_106766_56_0a59264a-c17f-4827-8aae-a370d6c3ad97//sanity-lnet.test_260.dmesg.\$(hostname -s).1723310919.log
onyx-141vm12: invalid parameter 'dump_kernel'

Server: RHEL 9.3/ldiskfs/5.14.0-362.24.1_lustre.el9.x86_64/2.15.65.6
Client: RHEL 9.3/5.14.0-362.24.1_lustre.el9.x86_64/2.15.65.6

+1 on Master https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_logs/8c4bb018-043b-460f-8314-9e18cb716006/show_text

It looks like this is now failing with "wrong value set" instead of the previous error, since 2024/08-08 when this patch landed:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/search?status%5B%5D=FAIL&test_set_script_id=a2b1c4b2-b449-11e9-b88c-52540065bddc&sub_test_script_id=991ad5aa-3ccc-4ad2-83a1-9244a8b8881b&start_date=2024-08-01&end_date=2024-08-10&source=sub_tests#redirect

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55910/
Subject: LU-18047 lnet: sanity-lnet test_260 wrong value
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 8e7a1404e190b1d339ea05bf00324082f15ea7f4

"Frank Sehr <fsehr@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55910
Subject: LU-18047 lnet: sanity-lnet test_260 wrong value
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 0aa4287473a6baa031640154b7e6b4757e93f984

Frank mentioned that this is likely a test script issue and the test script needs to add a short delay for the operation to complete, or preferably a polling check with wait_update() so that it doesn't wait longer than necessary.

It looks like this test has started failing intermittently since it was recently landed in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/53310 "LU-16959 lnet: auto-tune ARP-related sysctl setting" on 2024-07-17.

https://testing.whamcloud.com/search?status%5B%5D=FAIL&test_set_script_id=a2b1c4b2-b449-11e9-b88c-52540065bddc&sub_test_script_id=991ad5aa-3ccc-4ad2-83a1-9244a8b8881b&start_date=2024-07-13&end_date=2024-07-19&source=sub_tests#redirect
